Zencoder is an AI coding platform that goes beyond using a single AI to write code, it uses multiple AI agents working together to build apps and websites faster.

It can generate full applications by dividing the work between different AI agents like front-end, back-end, and database development.

When you give Zencoder a prompt to build an app or website, it automatically splits the task. One AI agent creates the front-end, another builds the back-end logic, and another manages the database.

What makes it powerful is that all these AI agents work at the same time. Instead of one AI doing everything step by step, Zencoder works like a team of AI developers building your project faster.

🚀 Agency Clients ↓

If you’re struggling to land high-paying clients,

it’s not because the market is bad.

It’s because you have no proof.

Clients don’t care about your skills.

They care about results.

Here’s the exact process I’d follow if I had to start from zero again 👇

1️⃣ Find the right people

Millions already have the problem you solve.

Your job is to find them first — not convince random people.

Build a list of 500–1000 ideal prospects (LinkedIn works best).

2️⃣ Remove money from the equation

No proof = no leverage.

So work free or low-ticket at the start.

Not forever — just until you can prove your solution works.

3️⃣ Document everything

Solve one real problem.

Then turn it into a clear case study.

Pain → process → result.

4️⃣ Reach out with proof

Send short, personalized Loom videos.

Show how you helped someone just like them.

Then invite them to a call.

5️⃣ Sell with confidence

Now selling becomes easy —

because you’re offering a proven solution, not promises.

6️⃣ Productize your service

Define what you deliver.

Fix the scope.

Set a no-brainer price.

No more custom chaos.

Just consistent delivery and scalable growth.

This is how agencies actually grow.

If you want my 30-day guide to signing your next agency client,

comment “AGENCY” and I’ll send it to you 👇

APP LAUNCH CHECKLIST

App Store Stuff

→ App title with keywords
→ Subtitle (don’t leave empty)
→ App description (hook in first 2 lines)
→ Keywords researched and added
→ Screenshots (show benefits, not features)
→ App preview video (optional but helps)
→ Privacy policy URL
→ Support URL
→ App category selected
→ Age rating completed

Website Stuff

→ Landing page live
→ Open Graph tags set (og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url)
→ Favicon added
→ Mobile responsive
→ SSL certificate active (https)
→ Download/CTA button working

SEO Stuff

→ Google Search Console connected
→ Bing Webmaster Tools connected
→ Sitemap submitted
→ IndexNow configured
→ Meta title and description set
→ Robots.txt file in place

Marketing Stuff

→ Launch post drafted
→ Social media assets ready (images, captions)
→ Email list notified
→ Product Hunt listing prepped (if using)
→ Friends/community ready to support

Legal Stuff

→ Privacy policy written and linked
→ Terms of service written and linked
→ Data handling documented
→ GDPR compliance (if applicable)
→ Cookie notice (if website tracks)

Save this. Use it. Don’t launch without it.

Combining Claude Code with NotebookLM is a complete cheat code that nobody's talking about

🔥 Comment "SETUP" for everything.

Here's how to set up your own research agent that costs you nothing and runs locally:

1. Clone the notebooklm-py repository from GitHub
2. Install the requirements with pip install -r requirements.txt
3. Run playwright install chromium to get the browser driver
4. Add the included skill file to your Claude Code setup
5. Start feeding sources to your new research agent

This combo gives you a research assistant that can pull together slide decks, infographics, and comprehensive reports in minutes. NotebookLM handles the RAG processing while Claude Code automates the entire workflow through the terminal.

The setup takes about two minutes and your data stays completely private since everything runs on your machine.

⏬ How to Get 100 Gym Leads Using AI

Here is exactly how I do it
Step 1 - Go to claude.ai
Create a free account if you don’t have one

Step 2 - Add Vibe Prospecting
Click the connections icon on the left sidebar, search “Vibe Prospecting” and click Connect

Step 3 - Type this prompt
“Find 100 gym owners and fitness decision makers in [your city or country] with their email and phone number”

Step 4 - Ask for contact details
Type: “Add email and phone numbers to all leads”

Step 5 - Download your list
Type: “Export all leads to CSV”

Click the download link and save the file

That’s it. 100 leads with name, email, phone and LinkedIn in under 5 minutes.

You can change “gym owners” to any niche - salons, restaurants, real estate agents and so on.

Save this for later and follow for more AI lead generation tips.

Comment 🔥 A few months ago, getting a single qualified lead used to take me hours.

Searching businesses on maps…
Checking their Instagram…
Analyzing their website…
Finding where they are lagging…
Then finally doing outreach.

That entire process taught me a lot about lead generation.

But recently I tested something interesting.

An AI tool that can generate business leads in seconds.

What used to take hours of manual research…
can now happen almost instantly.

And if you combine this with automation and proper follow-ups, it can become a powerful system for getting clients.

The digital space is evolving faster than ever.

⏬ Most people still open Canva, search templates and spend 30 minutes designing one post.

Now you can connect AI with Canva and create designs just by giving a prompt.

Instagram posts, YouTube thumbnails, logos, presentations, even a full brand kit can be generated in seconds. And the best part is it creates a real Canva file, so you can still edit fonts, colors and layouts anytime.

This is huge for content creators, marketers, agencies and small business owners who need designs almost every day.

But it will definitely affect certain roles.

Beginner graphic designers who mainly create simple Instagram posts or basic marketing creatives will feel the biggest impact.
Freelancers who sell low cost social media designs on platforms like Fiverr may see demand drop.
Even small agencies that only offer basic design services might struggle.

The demand will slowly move toward people who do branding, creative direction, storytelling and strategy not just simple designs.

Tools are changing fast. The real advantage will go to the people who learn how to use them early.

Resources I Followed to Clear SSC CGL (GST Inspector)

Personal Background
I cleared SSC CGL Exam and got selected as a GST Inspector.

Medium: Hindi Medium

Graduation: Civil Engineering

Maths background helped, but CGL Maths is different , as it needs specific strategy and practice.

---

Exam Overview & Cutoffs

SSC CGL 2023 was tough, and vacancies were low (~7800)

Prelims Cutoff (UR): ~150

Mains Cutoff (UR):

301 – Normal Posts

310 – 4600 GP Posts
(No normalisation in Mains as the exam was conducted in a single shift)

---

Subject-wise Books & Materials

Mathematics

Even with a maths background, SSC Maths requires exam-specific learning and practice.

Arithmetic:

•Aditya Ranjan Sir – Arithmetic Batch
•Bhutesh Sir – Tier 2 Batch (Arithmetic + Advanced Maths for mains)
•Bhutesh Sir’s Practice Sheets
•Abhinay Sir – Selected Mains-level classes on YouTube

---

Reasoning

•Mostly prepared from YouTube
•Reasoning Pinnacle (PYQs) – must for concept clarity and pattern understanding

---

General Awareness (GK)

•Approach: Emphasizes a systematic approach, dividing GS into five core subjects (History, Polity, Economy, Geography, Science) and a sixth section (Current Affairs & Miscellaneous)
•Made notes all 6 parts of GS from Lucent and multiple YouTube playlists from different teachers for coverage

Before Mains:

•Parmar Sir Batch – full syllabus revision
•Revised Parmar Sir’s notes multiple times

---

English has 3 parts

1. Grammar:

•Rani Ma’am – 16 lecture YouTube playlist
•Neetu Singh – Volume 1 (concepts + practice)

2. Vocabulary:

•Only Blackbook (no extra sources)

3. Reading Comprehension & Practice:

•Neon Publications 3 books for
 RCs
 Cloze Tests
 PQRS (Jumbled Sentences)

Final Advice:- Consistency, limited resources, and repeated revision mattered more than collecting books.

Hand Gripper Forearm workouts.

Baseball guys, is this legit? Guess which sport has the highest risk of shoulder injury - No suprise… Baseball. That might scare people away from training rotator cuffs, but I see it as a reason to intentionally prepare.

Prehab shoulder training and shoulder bulletproofing feels so anabolic. 

Specimin training requires mobility, size, and strength. I will not allow myself to become a stiff gym bro.

I believe this training will lead to longevity. Many people cringe at the sight, and assume I’ll be ruined in my middle ages. But I try not to overthink it: if it feels good, and improves my life quality, I’ll do more of it.

New research suggests that topical application of onion juice may stimulate hair regrowth in individuals with alopecia areata—a form of non-scarring, patchy hair loss. In a 2002 controlled trial, participants who applied crude onion juice twice daily for two months saw significant onset of terminal hair regrowth: after six weeks, 86.9 percent of the treated group exhibited new hair growth, compared to only 13 percent in the water-treated control group (P < 0.0001) . Nor did the regrowth differ significantly by sex (male 93.7%, female 71.4%) Hair regrowth began as early as two weeks in some cases and became evident in the majority by four weeks.

Key proposed mechanisms include sulfur‑rich compounds like quercetin that promote follicular nourishment, anti‑microbial effects that support scalp health, and antioxidant or anti‑inflammatory action that protects hair follicles from damage  However, the trial was limited in scope—small sample size, focused on alopecia areata, and without replication—so broader efficacy remains unconfirmed 

While not a cure‑all for genetic or pattern baldness, this study provides preliminary evidence supporting onion juice as a low‑risk, accessible adjunct in alopecia areata management. Consistent use—twice daily application over six weeks—may be key to observing potential benefits.

Study: Onion juice twice daily → 87 % regrowth at six weeks in alopecia areata cases.

Via @drericberg

New research suggests that topical application of onion juice may stimulate hair regrowth in individuals with alopecia areata—a form of non-scarring, patchy hair loss. In a 2002 controlled trial, participants who applied crude onion juice twice daily for two months saw significant onset of terminal hair regrowth: after six weeks, 86.9 percent of the treated group exhibited new hair growth, compared to only 13 percent in the water-treated control group (P < 0.0001) . Nor did the regrowth differ significantly by sex (male 93.7%, female 71.4%) Hair regrowth began as early as two weeks in some cases and became evident in the majority by four weeks.

Key proposed mechanisms include sulfur‑rich compounds like quercetin that promote follicular nourishment, anti‑microbial effects that support scalp health, and antioxidant or anti‑inflammatory action that protects hair follicles from damage  However, the trial was limited in scope—small sample size, focused on alopecia areata, and without replication—so broader efficacy remains unconfirmed 

While not a cure‑all for genetic or pattern baldness, this study provides preliminary evidence supporting onion juice as a low‑risk, accessible adjunct in alopecia areata management. Consistent use—twice daily application over six weeks—may be key to observing potential benefits.

Study: Onion juice twice daily → 87 % regrowth at six weeks in alopecia areata cases.

"matrix-escaping" tools.** 
---

### **📚 Must-Read Books** 
1. **"The 48 Laws of Power" – Robert Greene** *(Understand how manipulation works in society.)* 
2. **"Brave New World" – Aldous Huxley** *(A dystopian view of pleasure-based control.)* 
3. **"1984" – George Orwell** *(Surveillance, propaganda, and thought control.)* 
4. **"The Kybalion" – Three Initiates** *(Hermetic wisdom on mental mastery.)* 
5. **"The Power of Now" – Eckhart Tolle** *(Escaping the mind’s illusions.)* 
6. **"Be Here Now" – Ram Dass** *(Spiritual awakening & psychedelic insights.)* 
7. **"The Fourth Turning" – Strauss & Howe** *(Cycles of societal collapse & rebirth.)* 
8. **"Sapiens" – Yuval Noah Harari** *(How human systems control us.)* 
9. **"The Untethered Soul" – Michael Singer** *(Freeing yourself from mental chains.)* 
10. **"Reality Transurfing" – Vadim Zeland** *(Shifting your reality like the Matrix.)* 

*(Bonus: "The Matrix" screenplay – for symbolic study.)* 

---

### **🧠 Mental & Spiritual Techniques** 
1. **Lucid Dreaming** – Train yourself to become aware in dreams (a literal "matrix" escape). 
 - *Technique:* Reality checks (asking "Am I dreaming?"), dream journaling. 
2. **Meditation (Vipassana or Zen)** – Detach from programmed thoughts. 
 - *Start with:* 10 mins daily, focus on breath or silent awareness. 
3. **Astral Projection** – Conscious out-of-body experiences. 
 - *Method:* The "rope technique" (visualize pulling yourself out of your body). 
4. **Psychedelics (Responsibly!)** – Substances like psilocybin, DMT, or LSD can dissolve illusions. 
 - *Warning:* Only in safe, legal settings with intention. 
5. **Shadow Work** – Integrate suppressed parts of yourself (Carl Jung’s method). 
 - *How:* Journaling, analyzing triggers, facing fears. 

---

### **💡 Practical Lifestyle Shifts** 
1. **Digital Minimalism** – Delete social media, use ad-blockers, avoid algorithm traps. 
2. **Financial Independence** – Escape the 9-5 rat race (learn investing, side hustles, crypto). 
3. **Grow Your Own Food** – Break reliance on industrial systems.

Left or Right? Your breath is more powerful than you think…

In yogic and Vedic science, your breath alternates between the left nostril (Ida Nadi) and right nostril (Pingala Nadi) every 60 to 90 minutes.

This isn’t random—each side affects your energy, emotions, and outcomes differently.

🌙 Left Nostril Active = Ida Nadi
– Calming, intuitive, feminine, connected to Moon energy.
– Best for: Meditating, chanting mantras, spiritual practices, creative work, emotional conversations, introspection.
– Avoid: Intense workouts, business meetings, fast decision-making.

🌞 Right Nostril Active = Pingala Nadi
– Energizing, logical, masculine, linked with Sun energy.
– Best for: Presentations, physical tasks, problem-solving, goal execution, interviews, travel.
– Avoid: Meditation, mantra chanting, emotionally charged talks.

⚖️ Balanced Breath = Sushumna Nadi
– Rare and sacred: Indicates a high-energy, spiritually aligned state.
– Perfect for: Deep meditation, spiritual insight, rituals.

🧘‍♂️ Want to switch your breath to a specific side?
Here’s how:
👉 Lie down on your left side to activate your right nostril (Pingala)
👉 Lie on your right side to activate your left nostril (Ida)

💡 Bonus Trick: Block one nostril gently with your finger and breathe from the other for 2–3 minutes to activate it.

✨ Your breath holds the secret to your energy, mindset, and success. Use it consciously.

What’s your salary expectation?

Do not make the mistake of answering this question like this - “I need a 25% hike”.

Because if the number is way below their budget, you are leaving the money on the table.

So, instead, here is one of the ways you can answer this question - Hi happy to share my expectations, but I would love to hear from your side, what’s the budget you have for this role?

If the company don’t share the number, which is what is the case most of the time, here is what you need to do:

Step 1: Research - 

Either find the actual salary range for that role from websites such as 

glassdoor
ambition box
levels[.]fyi
payscale

or, connect with people working in the organisation and find the salary range.

Once you have clarity on the salary range, answer it like this - “I am currently getting interviewed at 2 other companies which are willing to give the salary within a range of X to Y lacs per annum. Is that something that is within your budget as well?”

Keep the range similar to the range coming out of your research.

you are underpaid, this will help you get a major correction in your salary.

- 1/2 tsp salt 
- 1/2 tsp black pepper powder

**For Manchurian Gravy:** 
- 1 tbsp oil 
- 1/2 cup capsicum, finely chopped 
- 1/2 cup onion, finely chopped 
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste 
- 2 tbsp tomato sauce 
- 1 tbsp soy sauce 
- 1 tbsp red chili sauce 
- 1 tbsp vinegar 
- Salt (as per taste) 
- 1/4 cup water

The only way to change your mood is to take action. Here's how:

1️⃣ Overthinking? → Write it down. Journaling helps declutter your mind and bring clarity. ✍️
2️⃣ Anxious? → Meditate. Deep breathing and mindfulness can calm your nerves. 🧘‍♂️
3️⃣ Tired? → Take a nap. A short rest can recharge your energy. 😴
4️⃣ Sad? → Exercise. Movement releases endorphins, the natural mood boosters. 🏋️‍♂️
5️⃣ Stressed? → Go for a walk. Fresh air and movement help relieve tension. 🚶‍♂️
6️⃣ Angry? → Listen to music. It can shift your emotions instantly. 🎧
7️⃣ Lazy? → Reduce screen time. Too much scrolling drains your motivation. 📱
8️⃣ Burnt out? → Read. A good book can refresh your mind. 📖

Take Actions —> Change your Mindset

High Protein Honey BBQ Chicken & Garlic Parmesan Potatoes🍗🧀🍟

The perfect balance of savoury, sweet & tangy! So much depth in flavour with even more protein thanks to the upgraded creamy sauce🤌🏽🔥

Macros Per Serving (5 Total)

578 Calories | 55g Protein | 50g Carbs | 17g Fat

Ingredients (Makes 5)

Honey BBQ Chicken:
- 1000g Raw Chicken Breast, cubed
- 2 Tsp Garlic Powder
- 2 Tsp Smoked Paprika
- 1.5 Tsp Cumin
- 1.5 Tsp Salt
- 2 Tsp Black Pepper
- 2 Tsp Olive Oil
- 30g Light Butter (for cooking all batches)
- 60g Honey (to coat all batches)
- 160g Sugar Free BBQ Sauce (Brand: G Hughes)
- 2 Tbsp Fresh Parsley, chopped

• Cook on medium heat for 5 mins per side till golden and crispy
• Lower the heat when adding honey & BBQ sauce. Mix for 1 min till well coated!

Garlic Parmesan Potatoes:
- 1000g Uncooked Potatoes, cubed
- 1.5 Tsp Salt
- 2 Tsp Italian Herbs
- 2 Tsp Smoked Paprika
- 1.5 Tsp Garlic Powder
- 1.5 Tsp Onion Powder
- 30g Grated Parmigiano Reggiano
- 2 Tsp Olive Oil (optional)

• Oven bake or air fry for 20-22 mins till golden and crusted

Creamy Sauce:
- 200g Fat Free Cottage Cheese, blended
- 300ml 1% Milk
- 1 Tsp Italian Herbs
- 1 Tsp Garlic Powder
- 40g Grated Parmesan

• When adding blended sauce to pan, make sure it’s low heat! Don’t need to cook the sauce

Here’s a protein-packed Egg Pizza in under 10 mins with free-range eggs from @farmmade_foods and leftover rotis. It’s a hit with kids and you’ll love how easy and quick it is to make!

Method: 
-Crack 3 free-range eggs in a bowl. 
-Add chopped onions, tomatoes & capsicum. 
-Add 1/2tsp turmeric powder, 1/2tsp red chilli powder, 1/4tsp pepper powder & salt to taste. 
-Add 2 leftover rotis chopped up as shown in the video. Mix well. 
-Take a pan, add some oil & pour in the mix. 
-Spread it evenly & cover it. 
-Flip it & top up with some onions, tomatoes, capsicum, corn & cheese. 
-Cover & cook for another 2min till the cheese melts. 
-Slice it & enjoy.

Here’s the workout:

1️⃣ Elephant Walks, 30 reps

2️⃣ Single Leg RDL, 10 reps each side

3️⃣ Single Leg Hamstring Stretch, 30 seconds per side

This quick routine will improve your lower body mobility and give you a better range of motion, both in the gym and in everyday life.

It only takes a few minutes, so no excuses! Perfect as warm-up or cool-down exercises too.

Why are some people able to regulate their emotions well whereas others are not able to? Focus on the nervous system.

Nervous system regulation is the link between the mind and the body. Mental and physical health can get impacted if we stay hyper aroused or hypo aroused all the time. Haven't you heard,being in the survival mode or the fight or flight mode is not good for the whole system.

Postpartum rage, postpartum anxiety, postpartum depression, or even psychosis is an indication that the mother needs help.

Not just during postpartum but even otherwise if you feel you are not able to be regulated for some reason, evaluate why and find out what tools can work for you.

Your nervous system fuels your immune system, hormonal system and digestive system. Learning to regulate your Nervous system regulation is the best thing you can do for holistic health. 💚

Counselors dealing with Maternal mental health should be trauma informed and need to give tools to support the mother's nervous system instead of just getting her drugs. That doesn't complete the puzzle & many moms I know were given meds when their situation only required other kinds of support & resources. 

There's a recorded session on Maya's Amma App to learn more about the nervous system or get in touch with your therapist now.

Happy regulation !!

Swati Jagdish
Psychologist
Sexuality health educator
.
.

High Protein Crispy Korean Popcorn Chicken! 🔥🍗🇰🇷By @panaceapalm 

ONLY 483 calories! Easy High Protein Meal Prep😋

Serves 4: 🍽️🍽️🍽️🍽️

Calories & Macros 📊
Per Meal: 495 calories 
50g P | 68g C | 3g F

Popcorn Chicken
- 800g chicken breast (I get mine from @musclefooduk use code PANACEA!)
- tbsp black pepper
- 20ml soy sauce
- 1 egg
- 40g cornflour/cornstarch

Airfry 200°C for 10-12 minutes
Oven Bake 220°C for 15-18 minutes (typo in video timings)

Korean Glaze 
- 60ml soy sauce
- 80ml water
- 2 tbsp low cal ketchup (brand: Heinz 50% less)
- 3 tbsp gochujang paste (found is most supermarkets)
- 2 tbsp rice vinegar
- 2 tbsp honey
- 30g brown stevia (brand: pure via)

Sweet Sticky Rice
- 200g uncooked sticky rice (brand: Tesco)
- 3 tbsp rice vinegar
- tbsp sweetener
- Salt to taste
Cook to instructions then allow to cool for 10 minutes before adding 3 tbsp of rice vinegar, a tbsp of sweetener and salt to taste, mix again

Garnishes
- Chopped green onions (green parts)
- Sesame seeds

Storage & Heating ♨️
- Store for up to 5 days in the fridge 
- To reheat, add a tsp of water and microwave for 3 minutes partially covered, once heated through mix well and enjoy!

၊၊||၊ KONNICHIWA SAYONARA - Nonoka Murakata a.k.a. Nono-chan (3-year old during this time)

Release Date: January 5, 2022
Genre: J-Pop • Children Song
Label: King Record Co., Ltd

💡 Nonoka Murakata, aged just three (during this time of the clip, she’s 6 y/o now), placed 2nd in Japan’s 35th Nursery Rhyme Children’s Song Contest. Born on May 31, 2018, she will debut with a CD on May 26 2022, becoming the youngest singer to do so. 

💡 Known as Nono-chan, she won the Silver prize in the children’s category last October 2022, setting a record as the youngest recipient. Her rendition of “Dog Policeman” has amassed nearly 15 million YouTube views since December.

💡 Standing at only 82cm tall, Nono-chan’s adorable gestures and flawless singing have charmed many. Celebrities like Kyary Pamyu Pamyu and JYJ’s Jaejoong have supported her, mentioning her on social media. Her upcoming mini album, “Nono-chan 2 Years Old Children’s Songs,” includes “Dog Policeman” and other nursery rhymes she loves.

💡 King Records has released behind-the-scenes footage of Nono-chan’s recording sessions, showing her in oversized headphones. With such early achievements, many are excited to see Nono-chan’s future in music.

Konderingi Malai,Dindigul, Tamil Nadu 📍 

Located in Dindigul district, Konderingi Malai stands tall at approximately 3,850 meters above sea level. 

For those looking to reconnect with nature, this is the perfect place.

Whether you’re a trekker or simply looking a peaceful place, this sivan hill will be an unforgettable experience. 

The way to the top is truly a challenge and takes you through lush greenery, accompanied by the pleasent sounds of nature, making this place to remember.

Don’t miss this incredible hill experience and the adventure and you won't regret it!

How to Reach:

By Road: Konderingi Malai is easily accessible by road. 
From Dindigul town, it’s about a 30-minute drive. You can park your vehicle at the base and start your trek from there.

By Train: The nearest railway station is Dindigul Junction, around 15 km away. From there, you can hire a taxi or take a local bus to reach the base of the hill.

By Air: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport, approximately 80 km away. From the airport, you can rent a car or take a bus to Dindigul and then drive to Konderingi Malai.

At the summit, the view is nothing short of breathtaking. 

The panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscapes are a reward for your efforts, especially if you time your visit for sunrise or sunset. 🌅

📍 Location: Konderingi Malai, Dindigul District
🚶‍♂️ Height: 3,850 meters above sea level
📸 Perfect For: Trekkers, Nature Lovers, Photographers

Episode 2 of #NoOvenNoProblem ☀️, a series where I make your favourite desserts at home WITHOUT an oven 💁🏻‍♂️ Making bread at home has never been easier 🥰 This Suji Bread in the Cooker is the simplest way to get the softest and fluffiest bread at home without any hassle ❤️

Ingredients 
🍞1+1/2 cup suji
🍞1 tsp instant yeast 
🍞1 tbsp castor sugar
🍞1/4 tsp salt
🍞3 tbsp milk 
🍞1/2 cup water 
🍞2 tbsp oil 
🍞3 tbsp mixed seeds, to top

Method 
1. In a blender jar add 1+1/2 cup suji and finely grind it.
2. Add finely ground suji, yeast, sugar and salt in a bowl and give it a good mix.
3. Now add in water, milk, oil and mix using a wooden spoon to form a batter.
4. Let the batter rest for 10 minutes.This will ensure that semolina has absorbed all the water.
5. After 10 minutes,transfer the dough onto a greased surface and knead it for around 15 minutes.
6. To check if the dough is ready to proof, take small piece of dough  and gently stretch it between your fingers . If the dough can be stretched thin enough and light passes through it , it means the dough is ready and it has passed the windowpane test.
7. Once the dough is formed,transfer it in a large bowl greased with oil. Cover the bowl with a cloth and let it proof for 1 hour in a dark warm place  or until doubled in volume.
8. Knock down the dough after the first proofing.
9. Roll out the dough into a rough  rectangle of 1/2 inch thickness.Then starting from one shorter end , roll in the dough for form a log. Make sure to pinch and lock after every turn.
10. Transfer this log In a greased 7X4 inch loaf pan. Let it proof for another 30 minutes or doubled in volume.
11. Meanwhile take a cooker set over medium heat and spread a layer of salt in it. Place a stand in middle and preheat for 10 minutes.
12. Once the dough has risen, brush some milk and sprinkle seeds on top.
13. Bake the bread in the preheated cooker for 25 minutes or until the top is golden brown.

There is a huge market for Pickle and you can kick start the business with minimum expenses.

There are various steps you will need to follow before you start a pickle business. They are:
1. Which Pickle to Produce:-
-The first step is to decide which type of pickle you want to produce. There are numerous varieties of pickles you can produce. Each variety of pickles has its own customer base.

2. Choose The Infrastructure For Manufacturing The Pickles
-If you are making the pickles at home, you will only need vessels to mix the ingredients and marinate the pickle, and the jars and containers to store them.

3. Arrange for the Registration and Licenses
*FSSAI
*GST Liscence
*Pan card registration 
*Business entity registration 

6. Packaging and Pricing the Pickles
-Before you sell the pickles to the consumer, you will need to focus on the packaging. You can either store your pickles in glass, plastic, or any other material equipped to store the finished product.
You will also need to price your product appropriately. You can’t price it too high or too low. If you price it too high, you will not be able to attract consumers; if you price it too low, people will get suspicious of the quality. 

7. Promotional Tools
-If you are running a home-based business, you just need to maintain direct contact with your customers and using WhatsApp will suffice.

8. Pickle Business Investment
-Home Business - An investment of around ₹10,000 in jars, utensils, and ingredients should be enough seed money.

Conclusion:-
-In short, pickle making business is a lucrative business opportunity that requires relatively little capital and labour. It is a great business you can start from the comfort of your home.
It has a good earning potential and hence, you can explore this option.

Feeling frustrated in your relationship?

Here are 3 ways you might be unknowingly enemies of your own love life:
 
🔹Mind Reader!  Expecting your partner to magically guess your needs is a recipe for disaster. Communicate openly! Tell them what you need, and listen when they do the same.  It's a two-way street, babe! ️

🔹The Silent Treatment Strikes!  Sweeping arguments under the rug might seem easier in the moment, but trust us, those issues will fester. ️ Talk things through, even if it's uncomfortable. Open communication builds stronger connections.

🔹Blended into One?  While quality time is key, don't lose yourself in the relationship. ‍Pursue your passions, nurture your friendships! Having your own life makes you a more interesting and fulfilled partner. ✨

Remember, relationships aren't perfect fairytales.  The pressure for everything to be flawless will only make things toxic. Understand your partner, communicate openly, and watch your love grow!

✅ 1. List your short-term (1-3 years), medium-term (3-5 years), and long-term (5+ years) financial goals & How you’re going to achieve them. Eg- travel, house, car, etc. 💸

✅ 2. Build an emergency savings of at least 3-6 months’ living expenses in a liquid mutual fund or a savings account. 💰✨Google “Emergency Fund calculator” & calculate the fund you need to save 

✅ 3. Maintain 3 bank accounts - personal accounts for you and your partner, plus a joint account (for combined expenses). Both partners should know how to manage their money!! 

✅ 4. Eliminate & try to pay off high-interest DEBTS first (credit cards, personal loans) to prevent interest accumulation. ❌

✅ 5. INSURANCE - Take health insurance for you and your family. 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Consider term life insurance for financial security of the family. ♥️

✅ 6. Start investing in a RETIREMENT ACCOUNT (e.g., PF or NPS)

✅ 7. INVEST in equities through mutual funds or direct stocks for long-term growth potential. 😃💰✨ Diversify investments across asset classes to manage risk.

✅ 8. Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) - Regularly invest for 5-10 years in equity mutual funds through SIP for disciplined approach. Begin with INR 500 or INR 2000 monthly. Increase as income rises. 📈🚀

✅ 9. Boost INCOME - Pursue part-time work, freelance gigs, or side projects. 👨‍💻💸 Use extra income to speed up debt repayment or increase investments.

📈💰✨ Share this with someone!

💬 Doubts and Growth: Addressing doubts can lead to a stronger bond.
🗣 Open Communication: Talking about difficult topics enhances connection.

🤝 Avoiding Complacency: Acknowledging feeling taken for granted helps grow together.
💖 Embrace Imperfections: Understanding each other’s flaws deepens love.
💃 Embrace Individuality: Avoiding pressure to conform nurtures authenticity.
😟 Overcoming Fear: Creating a safe space reduces fear of overreactions.
🗨 Effective Communication: Assess communication for a healthier relationship.
👩‍❤️‍👨 Trust and Boundaries: Discussing comfort levels around others builds trust.
🌠 Intimacy Desires: Exploring intimacy fosters a more satisfying connection.
These questions may feel uncomfortable, but they can lead to greater understanding and a stronger, more resilient relationship.

👉 Tip: Approach these conversations with empathy and openness.

These podcasts offer a treasure trove of knowledge for free that when implemented, have the ability to completely transform your life. ⠀
⠀
⠀
⁣1. Diary of a CEO: Multimillionare Steven Bartlett interviews successful leaders, sharing valuable insights on business and personal development. The best podcast to hear from different experts from a range of fields 9/10. 💼🎙️⠀
⠀
⠀
2. Science of Success: Use logic and analytics to optimise your dreams and work towards realistic goals. Evidence based personal growth, summed up by professionals 7/10. 🧪🔬⠀
⠀
3. Andrew Huberman: Dr. Andrew Huberman’s podcast explores the fascinating realm of mind and brain science, offering everyone a chance to unlock their full potential and understand the intricacies of human cognition 9/10. 🧠✨⠀
⠀
4. Lex Friedman: Lex Friedman’s thought-provoking conversations on technology, artificial intelligence, and the future. Some of the most profound and interesting conversations with a whole range of people 8/10. 🤖💡”⠀
⠀
5. Seneca: An extremely underrated revision tool for when reading and flashcards gets boring. Listen while going about your day and subconsciously absorb the info 7/10. ⏳⠀
⠀
⠀
6. The Passionate Few: Become motivated and empowered by the inspiring stories of successful individuals from various fields. This podcast is an invaluable resource for young people striving to chase their dreams and reach their full potential 8/10. 💪🎙️⠀
⠀
7. Jocko Willink: Learn the virtues of discipline, leadership, and resilience from former Navy SEAL Jocko Willink. His insights and experiences are essential for young individuals seeking personal growth and self-improvement. 🌟🇺🇸”⠀#explore #selfdevelopment #medstudent #productivity #studygram

Coding cheatsheet!!!
The best resources I found during my job switch journey were these SDE sheets. Solving problems randomly makes the path difficult to reach, because you won’t have a proper direction. 
I came across these SDE sheets while I was preparing for a switch and started solving those problems.
After solving 700+ problems in GeeksForGeeks and leetcode combined, I can definitely say that these problems cover most of the patterns.
1. Striver SDE sheet - Best sheet with all patterns but only drawback is, it is easy for revision and for ppl who are already comfortable solving easy level problems - https://takeuforward.org/interviews/strivers-sde-sheet-top-coding-interview-problems/
2. Love Babbar sheet - This covers most of the problems in Striver sheet and has extra problems which helps in covering basic patterns and then you can move to medium/hard patterns - https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/dsa-sheet-by-love-babbar/amp/

19 ChatGPT Prompts to build startup in 2023:

1. Business Audit
Prompt: "Act as a business consultant and conduct a comprehensive audit of my current business model, including strengths and areas for improvement."

2. Research Competitors

Prompt: "Act like a market research consultant. Provide me with a detailed analysis of my top five competitors in the [specific industry]."

3. Brainstorm Feature Idea

Prompt: "Imagine you're a product design consultant. Based on current market trends, suggest innovative features I can incorporate into my product."

4. Brainstorm Taglines

Prompt: "I want you to think like a brand expert. Brainstorm catchy taglines that encapsulate the essence of my business."

5. Keyword Research

Prompt: "Act as an SEO specialist. Provide me with keywords relevant to my business domain and their search volume."

6. Assess Pricing

Prompt: "Pretend you're a pricing strategist. Analyze the best pricing strategy for my product based on its features and the current market scenario."

7. Consider Other Costs

Prompt: "As a business consultant, highlight potential hidden costs involved in running my business."

8. Create Target Persona

Prompt: "Assume the role of a market researcher. Help me create a detailed customer persona for my product."

9. Brainstorm Target Markets

Prompt: "Act as a market expansion expert. Suggest potential markets or demographics I should target next."

10. Estimate Market Size

Prompt: "Pretend you're a data analyst. Help me estimate the market size for [specific product/service] in [region/country]."

11. Brainstorm Marketing Channels

Prompt: "Envision yourself as a marketing guru. Recommend the most effective marketing channels for my business."

12. Identify Revenue Streams

Prompt: "Think like a financial strategist. Highlight potential revenue streams for my business model."

13. Content Calendar

Prompt: "Operate as a content strategist. Help me draft a content calendar for the next three months."

14. Expected Profit-Loss

Prompt: "Act like a finance expert. Predict the profit and loss scenario for my business over the next year based on current metrics."

15. SWOT Analysis

Yoga Nidra or yogic sleep is a state in which conscious mind subsides but awareness remains. It brings deep relaxation for body while one can be consciously aware in the dream state. 

👉 1 hour of yoga Nidra can give the relaxation of few hours of regular sleep. 

👉How To Practice Yoga Nidra 

 1️⃣ Lie down in Shavasana 
2️⃣ Consciously relax whole body part by part 
3️⃣ Now concentrate on your natural breath. 

In the initial stages, you might fall asleep. But With practice the conscious mind drops away and subconscious mind comes to surface, while maintaining the awareness. 

In this state one is conscious of their dreams (consciousness in dream state). And deeper still, conscious awareness is maintained in DEEP SLEEP STATE.

𝗢𝗻𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗕𝗵𝗮𝗷𝗶 / 𝗣𝗮𝗸𝗼𝗿𝗮 / 𝗣𝗮𝗸𝗼𝗱𝗮 🧅 (𝗖𝗿𝗶𝘀𝗽𝘆 𝗼𝗻𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗳𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿𝘀) 

*I mislabeled the besan flour to suji or semolina flour in the video. Please disregard and use chickpea flour for this 😅

𝗜𝗡𝗚𝗥𝗘𝗗𝗜𝗘𝗡𝗧𝗦

𝗢𝗻𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗳𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿:
- 1 large white & red onion thinly sliced
- 2 tbsp coriander/cilantro (chopped)
- 1 green chilli chopped
- 1 tsp turmeric/garam masala/kashmiri chili powder
- 2 tsp salt
- 3/4 cup besan (chickpea flour)
- 1/4 cup rice flour
- water as needed

𝗥𝗮𝗶𝘁𝗮 (𝘆𝗼𝗴𝘂𝗿𝘁 𝘀𝗮𝘂𝗰𝗲):
- 1 cup plain unsweetened yogurt (curd)
- 1-2 cucumbers grated 
- 2 tbsp mint chopped
- 2 tbsp coriander chopped
- 1/2 lime juice
- Garam masala (optional)
- Salt to taste

𝗜𝗡𝗦𝗧𝗥𝗨𝗖𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡𝗦:
- Combine ingredients listed for the onion fritters. Add water a little at a time to form a semi thick batter. Fry at 165C until crispy and golden. Set on a paper towel/wire rack.
- To make yogurt sauce combine ingredients listed for raita. Make sure to squeeze the cucumbers dry using cheese cloth or a kitchen towel so that the mixture stays thick.
- Serve your onion fritters with the yogurt sauce and enjoy ❤️

6 tools every jobseeker should be using for interview preparation:

- Interview Warmup, by Google

An AI-based platform to help individuals practice interviewing. Asks questions, transcribes verbal responses in real-time, uses ML to give feedback.

- Pramp

Practice live interviews with peers. Pramp also specializes in free, mock code interviews.

- LinkedIn Interview Prep

I really like their ‘common questions’ database. This tool also uses AI to analyze your answers, you can also record yourself on video.

- Glassdoor Company Interview Questions

Glassdoor keeps a running list of interview questions candidates are currently being asked, organized by job and company.

- Meet Practice Meeting

This one is simple. Practice interviewing in video. Get comfortable with seeing yourself talking. Dry run in a practice session first.

- InterviewBuddy

Create a profile, choose a mock interview time slot, receive a curated list of prep materials for your target firm, get feedback and a recording of your practice interview.

Above all, practice and get feedback.

Give yourself enough time to improve where you need it.

Happy interviewing!

6 steps to get a data analyst job from scratch [Tech and Non-Tech]

Follow @onestopdata for more such content and check link in BIO for my course on PowerBI starting 28 may and workshop on cracking jobs and interviews in data analytics!

Comment 🔥 if you liked it🙌🏻

1) Learn the Technical Skills required

-Excel- For strong foundation
-PowerBI or Tableau- Data visualization
-SQL- Store, manage, and retrieve data
-Python or R- Processing/cleaning/analyzing data [This is optional and can be done later especially for non-IT people]

Free Resources in the guide section of this page.

2) Work on hands-on Projects to practice and build proof of work

Go to kaggle, data.gov, github websites etc and get datasets and practice your learnings.

You can also do guided projects (shared on my feed)- Alex the Analyst YouTube channel is great for such projects!

Projects are very important! Do diverse projects- Pick up different domains such as sales, marketing, tech, HR etc to get an overall idea.

 3) Certifications

Certifications are not necessary but can help enhance your resume (only after Projects). My recommendation would be Google/IBM certi or doing official certifications from Microsoft/salesforce etc.
 
4)Practice soft skills

Soft skills such as presentation, communication, teamwork, critical thinking etc. are important as a data analyst. Practice them by giving mock presentations and recording yourself.

5)Update your Linkedin Profile and Resume

Update your Resume with your skills and experiences, make sure it is ATS compliant. Use resumeworded.com to get sample templates.

Optimize your Linkedin profile to showcase your skills and projects and network with people.This way you will create connections and get referrals.

6) Apply for Jobs and prepare for Interviews

Find jobs through referrals, online portals such as Linkedin, glassdoor, indeed etc.
Practice interview questions from Leetcode, Stratascratch, Datalemur, Interview Query.

You can also find jobs as a BI/data viz analyst (Only requires Excel, PowerBI/tableau and SQL) or jobs such as sales analyst/HR analyst in your domain as a non-tech person.

My grandparents rarely chucked away food as it could always be transformed into something valuable like the many recipes I found in my grandma’s recipe book, passed on from generation to generation and now to me so I can share it with all of you in the hope they will bring the same joy!

COURGETTE PATTIES RECIPE:

- 600g x courgette 
- 40g x gram /chickpea flour 
- 50g x water 
- 1 x garlic clove 
- Bread crumbs as you like 
- Salt 
- Pepper

*Serve with a base of tomato sauce and vegan cheese 

APPLE PECTIN:

- Any kind of apple, including peel, etc.
- Add enough water to make them float
- Boil for 2 hours
- Strain & simmer until it reduces 
- Can in a sterile jar

CARROT TOPS REGROWN:

- add a thin layer of water to a flat bowl
- Replace water every 2 days
- Cut green at any size 

PESTO INGREDIENTS:

- 1 x Cup Carrot green shots
- 1/2 x cup Pine nuts
- 1/3 x Cup Olive oil
- Salt & pepper

This is  your sign to taste this flavour bomb from Erode 💣

It is the best selling item in every restaurant in the city of Erode. That's how delicious this is. 

Check out this recipe at the Cookd App or  cookdtv.com (LINK IN BIO)

Japan Chicken Recipe: 
Chicken (Boneless) - 400 gram

Batter:

Corn Flour - 4 tbsp
Maida - 4 tbsp
Black Pepper Powder - ½ tsp
Salt - ½ tsp
Water - As Needed
Oil - To Deep-fry

Creamy Sauce:

Butter - 2 tbsp
Green Chilli (slit) - 2 nos
Garlic (finely chopped) - 4 cloves
Milk - 1 ½ Cups
Salt - To Taste
Sugar - 3 tsp
Cashew - 20 nos
Coriander Leaves - 1 tsp

Instructions:

1. In a mixing bowl, add maida, cornflour, salt, pepper and water. Mix to form a batter to deep-fry the chicken pieces.

2. Add chicken pieces to the batter.

3. Heat oil in a deep frying pan and deep-fry the chicken pieces until it is cooked. Keep Aside.

4. Grind the cashews into cashew powder using a mixer grinder.

5. Heat a pan, add butter, once the butter melts, add garlic and slit green chilli. Saute for a minute.

6. Add the milk, salt and sugar. Stir and simmer for 2 minutes.

7. Add the cashew powder and let the sauce thicken. 

8. Add the deep-fried chicken pieces to the sauce.

9. Stir fry until the sauce coats the chicken pieces.

10. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot as an appetizer.

Easy Chicken Roast

Other recipes link in bio

Recipe

Ingredients (Serves 4)

1. Chicken - 3/4 kg,cut into medium-small pieces

2. Onion - 1 medium, finely chopped (Makes about 1-1.5 cup)

Ginger-garlic paste - 1.5 tbsp

Green chilies 1-2, finely chopped

Curry leaves - A few, torn

Turmeric powder - 1/2 tsp

(optional)

Kashmiri chilly powder (good quality) - 3-4 tsp

 Pepper powder -1/2 tsp

Salt To taste

3. Garam masala powder - 3/4 tsp

4. Cilantro Gi3 tbsp chopped

5. Oil 2-3 tbsp

Method
1. Marinate chicken pieces with ingredients numbered 2. Set aside for 30 minutes. 2. Transfer the chicken pieces to a pan. Add a tbsp
of oil, mix well and cook covered over medium heat for 12-15 minutes, stirring in between. Open the lid. Cook for 5-7 minutes over medium heat until moisture is mostly evaporated. Now add 1.5-2 tbsp oil, garam masala powder and continue cooking until chicken is completely done and slightly roasted. Sprinkle chopped cilantro and mix well. Switch off. Sprinkle lemon juice, while serving. Enjoy with chapathi/pulao etc.

Caramel Custard 🍮
Ingredients For Caramel:
½ cup Sugar
2 tbsp Water
For Pudding Mixture:
 500 ml Milk 
½ cup Sugar
4 Eggs
1 tsp Vanilla essence 
For the caramel :
heat the sugar and water together until it dissolves and turns into a deep golden color.
➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖
🔥Caramel making tips
Use Medium flame 
Do not use Low or High flame
If you use low flame ,Crystal will form
If you use high flames, it will burn 🔥
➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖
Transfer it to pan. Swirl the pan so that the bottom is evenly coated.
Set aside to let it solidify.
For Pudding Making :
In a pan, heat milk and sugar. It should become hot but should not boil.
Let it cool completely.
In another bowl, beat the eggs. Add the milk a little at a time while whisking continuously. 
add vanilla essence and mix well
Strain the mixture through a fine strainer. 
Strain 2 to 3 times for smooth texture 
Pour it on the solidified caramel.
Cover it with aluminum foil.
Place it in a steamer or a pot with adequate water and a metal stand on the bottom.
Steam for 30 minutes on medium heat. 
Check if it is done by pressing it gently with your fingers. It should bounce back lightly.
Remove the pan from the steamer and chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours, or for longer.
Flip the custard pudding onto a plate.
Serve and Enjoy 😋😍
✴️Cooking Tips:
If you are baking it in an oven, place the covered pudding in a tray of water. Bake at 180°c for 40 minutes, in a preheated oven.

Without egg ingredients

1 cup Milk
1 cup Condensed Milk 
1 cup Thick Curd 
1 tbsp Custard Powder 
1 tsp Vanilla Essence
 In a bowl take milk, condensed milk, curd, custard powder and vanilla essence. Mix everything well to get smooth mixture. Pour the mixture in the prepared caramel mould.

The old reel rule was only 1 condition rule. It was different.
But this rule is a 5 condition rule.

These rules are written by financial experts by taking assumptions & too many conditions. But the motive is to check if you can live stressful life after taking a big house.

Buying the first home is a dream and for cracking the right deal, from deciding the location to doing the paperwork, numerous aspects have to be considered to ensure a hassle-free home purchase. 
In India, many first-time homebuyers rely on home loans to buy their dream home. 
As home loans span over 20-30 years, they are counted as a long-term liability that involves investing a significant share of an individual’s income.
With the majority of first-home buyers in India belong to an age group of 30-40 years, which is one of the most crucial years in terms of both personal and professional growth, it becomes even more important to make an informed decision.

Do you want to know if you can afford a house? 
There’s a rule in finance, that is 5/20/30/50 rule.
If you follow this rule, you may buy a smaller house (or in a more distant suburb – price depends on location as we all find out in life) but you will surely have a more peaceful possession and live a life of less stress. 
That is a guarantee. 
Many people realized recently that their house was too big a committment – and the car added to the misery.

But what are the rules? 
Well, the rules are explained in the video.

Are you planning to buy a car?
Do you find this post helpful?

In this post, we have discussed the costs associated with a Car Loan and how you can save for your car using Mutual funds and also my view on which option is better so that you can make an informed choice.

Taking a Car Loan: The Real Cost
Car Loans are offered by all major banks in India and as it is secured by the car, the interest rate offered is lower than that of unsecured loans such as personal loans. However, there are other costs though small, that add up to increase the overall costs.
There are costs like document charges, processing fees, GST on that processing fee and so on.
 
Buying a Car with Loan: How Much More Are You Spending?
Before you even take a car loan you need to arrange the down payment. Most banks give you 80% to 90% of the car’s on-road price as a loan. This means you need to pay 10% to 20% from your own pocket at the time of purchasing the car. So if you take a loan for a car with an on-road price of Rs. 12 lakh, you will have to make a down payment of Rs. 1.2 lakh to Rs. 2.4 lakh.
Now, as we said earlier, the interest on a Car Loan is the primary cost that you incur over and above the cost of the car itself. The interest amount you will end up paying in total depends on the loan amount, interest, and loan tenure. Most banks today offer car loans with a tenure of up to 5 years, however, some lenders offer Car Loan tenure of up to 7 years.

♦️BOOK OVERVIEW♦️
 
In the stock market, we often assume that higher risk will give higher returns and lower risk will give you lower returns.

In short, it is a myth that you need to take more risks to get higher returns, but  Mohnish pabrai shared some eye-opening details in his book "THE DHANDHO INVESTOR."

He shares 9 principles of low-risk high-value methods to high return which are largely influenced by the investment philosophy of Warren Buffett and Charlie Munger.

The principles shared in this book are quite practical and easy to apply in the real world. It is a must-read for those who are interested in long-term investing in a business.

Some of the Principles are as follows- 

1. Buy a business that is already established.

2. Buy a business with an ultra-slow rate of change.

3. Buy a business with competitive advantages.

4. Bet heavily when odds are in your favor.

Today is my last day at the office 🥹

The Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972 is an Indian law that makes certain industries pay a one-time gratuity to retired or those who have resigned or deceased employees(to their Nominee). The law applies to railways, ports, factories, oilfields, plantations, mines, shops and establishment.

To be eligible for receiving Gratuity you must have worked in the organisation for at least 5 years and at least 240 days in the last one year.

The maximum amount of Gratuity payable by a Employer is 20 lacs

The Gratuity amount becomes payable within 30 days of becoming due

Neelam / Neela - Blue 
Vaanam - Sky
.
Neela vaanam - Blue sky
Niram - Color
Nirangal - Colors
.
A note about Neelam vs Neela:
.
When the word is NOT in front of the object, we use "neelam"
Sky color blue = Vaanam niram neelam.
.
When the word is in front of the object it is describing, we use "neela".
Blue Sky = neela vaanam
.
.
Neeyum naanum - You and I
Neeyum - You too
Naanum - Me too
.

All the meals mentioned in the video have the following nutrients that are essentials for THICKER HAIR:-

1.Omega-3 benefits for hair

provides essential proteins and nutrients to hair follicles and skin. prevents hair follicle inflammation — a factor that can directly contribute to hair loss. promotes circulation in the scalp that may trigger hair growth.

2. Biotin

If you’ve looked into hair growth vitamins in the past, you’ve probably run across biotin, also called B7, or vitamin H. Biotin helps the body break down protein, which is essential for hair and nail growth.
Most people get their biotin naturally through meat, so vegans and vegetarians are at particular risk for deficiency and may benefit from a B7 vitamin.

3. Protein

While most people associate protein with muscle growth and development, it’s also what makes up most of our hair. Protein is a macronutrient, which means that, unlike vitamins and minerals, our bodies require large amounts of it.

4. Vitamin C

We already know the balance between free radicals and antioxidants is important to hair health, since an excess of free radicals can damage hair. Vitamin C has powerful antioxidant properties that can help limit oxidative stress from free radicals. Our bodies also use vitamin C to produce collagen, which is a protein that plays an essential role in hair structure.

So you're thinking of purchasing a new car 🚗 and want to know what's the cheapest way to do it? If your car price is less than 15 lakhs and you're in the 30% income bracket then I have a great hack for you.

➡️ Car subscription!! 🚗

This is a new business model where you can subscribe to a car without owning it in exchange for a monthly fee. The world is moving to a more nomadic lifestyle where we do not want to be tied to just one place. 🏔 

For eg, I could work out of Bangalore for 1 year and then move to Meghalaya for another. Thus owning a car in my name becomes difficult to navigate between cities 🏙 Also, I do not want to pay the down payment, insurance and maintenance charges. Hence, a car subscription model would be ideal for me. Moreover, I can get up to 30% rebate on the car rental expenses 💰 making it even cheaper. If I do decide to buy the car after the rental period I can choose to do it for the depreciated value. 

➡️ Bottom line: For vehicles below 15 lakhs the math turns out to be cheaper if you rent and buy the car after 3 years assuming that you're in the 30% income bracket.

➡️ Source: Times of India

Aloe vera hand soap is just incredibly good for your skin and hair! It’s a great moisturiser but also helps to fight skin ageing, reduce infections, acne and boost the healing effects of wounds!

This is a simple yet really effective way of reducing the amount of plastic containers chucked away every week in your house by producing your stock of 100% natural hand soap!

RECIPE:

- 5 x Leaves Aloe Vera
- 1/2 x Jar Dry Lavender
- 1 x Tsp E-oil
- Food grade colour (optional)
- 1 kg Natural Soap Base

NOTES:

- When harvesting aloe Vera, let sit in water for 10-15 mins to remove the yellow latex
- You could do this same process by using LYE but I don’t like it as you lose some properties of your natural ingredients

Shapes influence how people feel about your brand ⏬

It's fascinating how shapes have an impact on how people see your brand. 

When building your brand it's useful to know what these shapes means and the effect they have on customers perception. 

Alongside the shapes you choose for your brand it's important that you still give your brand meaning and not let the shapes do all the talking.

Lets say you want people to feel trust towards your brand, let your actions speak louder than words and shapes. 

Over time people will relate your brand shape with that feeling of trust. 

Actions will always hold more influence over people's perception 🙂

The shape makes it easy to remember 😉

Pair the two together for a powerhouse of a brand 😍

Hello Makkale(People), Having one Cast Iron Paniyaram Pan is everyone’s Dream. Even me🤣. Many were still thinking of switching from Non-stick to Iron. Because Iron Pans need a little extra care. But Trust me, maintaining the iron pans or Kadai is very easy, not a kamba soothiram though. Little understanding is needed then it will become our favorite thing in the kitchen. 
 
📝How to Season Cast Iron Paniyaram Pan,

STEP:1 Soak the paniyaram pan in rice starch water(sadam vadicha Kanji) overnight. Next-Day You will see the red color rust. Use any dish wash liquid or soap scrub and Clean it using a metal scrubber. Completely dry it. This step I skipped because my Pan is Pre-seasoned. I don't want to harm it anymore. If your iron pan is not seasoned then you do the 1st step and then proceed with the 2nd step.

STEP: 2 Now heat the pan and add oil generously to each pit (You can use the used oil also), add chopped onions and roast it. Remove the onions, and repeat the same step one more time. then wipe it using a cloth or tissue. Now the Pan is ready to make Paniyaram. 

If you still feel that it’s not properly seasoned you can make the first batch of paniyaram with old idli batter and then try with a new batter.

✅️Do’s & ⛔️Don’t,

🔥Eggs, oil, paneer or any high fat items are friendly to iron pans so cook these ingredients frequently.

🔥Once Seasoned no need to use soap and wash every time after use, just wipe it with tissue and apply oil, and keep it. As we are heating the pan every time, it will get sterilized. 

✅️Chola( Sorghum) Paniyaram recipe, sweet and savory versions available with step by step pics and Video on blog www.premascook.com and youtube channel Prema’s Culinary Please Check.

✅️Have you tried this method before??? Or what other method do you use to season the Cast Iron Pans?

Personal Finance Post #46 – The Minimum Amount Due Credit Card trap

I have repeatedly told on this page that Credit card is one powerful financial tool –  I get so many amazing offers, free airport lounge access, reward points etc.

But how do Credit card companies fund these? They aren’t here to run charity. 
Do they only get it through the tall late fees they charge on defaults?

 No, if you have ever seen a credit card statement, you would find something called Minimum Due Amount, along with Due amount. 

What is that?
“The minimum amount due on a credit card is the minimum amount you are required to pay, on or before the payment due date, to ensure that you do not have to pay late fees. It is typically 5% of the total amount due”

If you pay this, you will not be considered as a defaulter and your credit score would be safe. 

How min. amount due works : 
If you, say spend 1L on your card, and pay only 5k, that’s your Min. Due amount, for the rest 95k, a massive ~40% interest will be charged – not from the defaulting date, but from the date you actually made those purchases.

Till you pay it back, that remaining amount will also be deducted from your credit limit, there by reducing your purchasing power and also in turn affecting your credit score (High credit utiliztation)

SO, if you are a follower of this page, take an OATH right away that you will never fall into this trap and will always pay the due amount fully – FOR THAT YOU NEED TO SPEND ONLY AS MUCH AS YOU CAN PAY FULLY.

Let’s learn together, Earn together.

Disclaimer : I am not a financial advisory, whatever I say here on my profile is purely for educational purpose. Please do your own reasearch and  consult your financial advisor before taking any decision.

Adding a message or InMail is the easiest way to stand out on LinkedIn. 

Example: 
1️⃣ Hiring Manager 
"Hi [Name], I noticed that you are a recruiter in the [name of the city] area. I wanted to reach out to discuss potentially working together. I'm a [share your experience] and currently seeking new opportunities. If you have time, I'd love to discuss whether my background would make me a fit for any openings you have.

2️⃣"Asking for help."
Hey James, I hope you're doing great. I came across your profile while looking for people who moved from consumer FMCG to the Software industry. Your career journey from Nestle to SaaS Startup really inspired me. It would be very helpful if you could suggest what resource helped you to transition? 

3️⃣"Leave a great first impression."
Hi Mr Bond, I hope you had a great week! I've been loving your content on Youtube, especially your recent post on "XYZ". I just wanted to connect so I could stay up to date with your posts on LinkedIn as well. 

References: 
➡️Personal Experience 
➡️Psychology today - Why we love talking about ourselves by Samantha Boardman M.D.
➡️LinkedIn connection guide by Cultivated Culture

Here's is the recipe for #GingerBeer. Perfected by @thispointer

To brew 4.5L you will need 
5L water
Ginger - 125g
Yeast - 5g (brewers' yeast preferred) .
Brown Sugar - 500g
Tea - around 6-7 table spoons
Spices like cloves, cinamon, cardamom
Black Raisins and lime/lemon juice

Preparation - Sanitize everything! Rinse with heated water 2-3 times. Make sure plastic bottles are not washed with warm water.

Make ginger paste - Grind ginger + 100-150ml of water + spices.

Make ginger tea - Take 2 - 2.5L of water, in a vessel. Add 6-7 tablespoons of tea + 500g sugar + 10 chopped black raisins. Get it to boil. Let it boil for around 10-15 minutes. Strain the tea. COOL IT DOWN completely. Need the yeast to do its magic.

Hydrate the yeast*- Take around 80-100ml of water. Warm it for 10 seconds in the microwave. Add 5g of yeast to the water. Let is activate

Put it all together
Take the 5L bottle/dispenser. Pour the ginger tea + hydrated yeast (make sure you get every bit) +  and add it in. 10-15 black raisins +  bit of lime/lemon juice for flavour + remaining water. Leave at least 300-500ml space from the top. Give it a shake. Cover the dispenser with muslin cloth. Store this bottle in a dark corner

IMPORTANT: if you close it all the way, the bottle will EXPLODE! 

Waiting game
You should be able to see bubbles, Depending on the yeast used, this could start in 24-36 hours. Basically the yeast has started consuming all the sugars in the mix.

Taste it after 2 days, you will know when ready. Add  brown sugar if you prefer. Keep it for a day. 

Filter the brew and start bottling your brew. Use plastic bottles. It helps to understand the carbonation levels. Once you bottle, check carbonation every half hour. Basically the bottles will inflate and become tight. Dont fill the bottle completely. Leave 4-5 cm from the neck. Once it is very firm, refrigerate it. 

Keep it for a couple of hours or days

Open with caution. Expect a lot of froth when you open. 

Disclaimer: I am no expert but have learned these things by watching/reading

Getting Internships are hard and there's no denying that. Even when our profiles are upto the mark but still we don't get a response at all. 💥

At this point there are 2 options.
1. Either we sit and cry
2. Or we look for alternatives.

I'm here for your no. 2. 👀

These are the easiest ways to compensate for an internship in your resume -

1. Startup projects like campus ambassador, student partner etc are the easiest to get.
2. Govt internships are not that easy to get but are always worth a try as some are genuinely very easily available.
3. Live projects. Learnt a course? Build things around it. Ex - Learnt financial modeling? Make financial models.
4. Research papers on what you've studied. Studied finance? Make it on behavioural finance or stock market. Google many are available.
5. Do courses that offer an internship post completion.

Got any questions? 💡
Feel free to ask 👇❤️

Book names here ⬇️

A Man’s Search for Meaning
Taught me how to live life even if one loses everything. How to still find meaning and happiness.

The 4 Agreements
Taught me how to cut down all the negativity from life by making 4 simple promises to myself.

The Courage to be Disliked 
Taught me how to follow my passion and overcome comparison and the fear of what will people say.

Letters from a Stoic
Taught me a lot about how to live life and handle my emotions during difficult times.

These books have made mental strong over the years as they talk about some really powerful life philosophies that frees you from so many lies that you tell yourself everyday.

While we consider dogs to be members of our family, feeding them the same food we eat can cause injury to them. Dogs are not used to eating the oily, fatty foods that we do, and they can get diarrhea and upset stomachs from them. It’s important to know what foods are toxic to dogs and avoid them.

Take a look at this and always make sure what youre giving your pup is safe for them!

When does a salad started getting yummy ?
Chicken Pasta Salad is super easy to make and packed with deliciousness. It’s healthy, light and scrumptious snack with your favourite sauce and  @weikfieldind Pasta of your choice 😀

Ingredients:

1 ltr Water 
½ tsp Salt
1.5 cup Pasta
4 tbsp Olive Oil
1 tsp Garlic
200 gm Chicken
3 tsp Pasta Seasoning
2 tsp Salt
2 tbsp Mustard Sauce
1 tbsp Red Chilli Sauce
1 tsp Parsely
2 tsp Vinegar
½ cup Capsicum
½ cup Red Bell Pepper
¼ cup Olive
½ cup Cherry Tomatoes

Method:

In a pan, boil 1 ltr Water into that add ½ tsp of Salt & 1.5 cup Pasta. Boil it for 9 minutes. After that drain the excess water from it.
_
Preheat the pan and add 1 tbsp Olive Oil, 1 tsp Garlic, 200 gm Chicken, 2 tsp Pasta Seasoning, and 1 tsp salt. Shallow fry the chicken from both sides. Make small pieces of it
_
In a bowl add 3 tbsp Olive Oil, 2 tbsp Mustard Sauce, 1 tbsp Red Chilli Sauce, 1 tsp Pasta Seasoning, 1 tsp Salt, 1 tsp Parsely, 2 tsp Vinegar. Mix it well.
_
Now add this into boiled pasta also you have to add ½ cup Capsicum, ½ cup Red Bell Pepper, ¼ cup Olive, ½ cup Cherry Tomatoes & Roasted Chicken pieces. Merge it well.
_
Serve the delicious and healthy Chicken Pasta Salad!

Roasted Coconut Chutney..! An easy flavourful Chutney goes well with Boiled Rice..!! Grated coconut fresh or frozen -150gm
Dried red chillies - 4
Shallots - 4
Tamarind dried - gooseberry size
Salt to taste
Coconut oil -20ml
Curry leaves- few

In a frying pan slow roast red chillies shallots and tamarind till the Chilly get slight smocky black in colour add 10 ml coconut oil and on a low flame slow roast the coconut till slight brown add salt. In a blender make a corose powder without any water and move in to a bowl add curry leaves and coconut oil and mix well. Check seasoning.

When staff found an abandoned egg which the other vultures would not care for, they decided to give it to the male vultures.
It was the zoo's first successful hatching in five years.
The second vulture was raised by heterosexual parents being cared for in captivity after they were hurt in a road accident in Spain. .
The two birds hatched in Amsterdam in April and May 2017. One of them was raised from an egg by a male vulture couple.
Zoo keeper Job van Tol last year described the pair as "a very tight couple".
"We have had them for some years. They always build a nest together, bond and mate together," he told the BBC.
.
A vulture raised by gay parents has been released into the wild as part of a conservation programme.
